expectations.text: Has a case for .documentExpired with a distinct user-facing message about the document being expired

expectations.text: Has a case for .holderUnderage with a distinct user-facing message about the age requirement
expectations.evidence: There is no `.holderUnderage` case in `didReject`. The underage message is shown in `didCapture` via a manual age calculation, not as a rejection reason handler.

expectations.text: Has a case for .timeout with a distinct user-facing message about the capture timing out
expectations.evidence: Line 123: the ternary checks `reason == .timeout` and returns `Constants.Message.timeout` ("Document capture failed..."), which is distinct from the fallback message.

expectations.text: Has a default case for all other rejection reasons
expectations.evidence: No switch statement exists in `didReject`, so there is no default case. The ternary fallback handles only the non-timeout path but is not a switch default.

expectations.text: Does NOT use rejectHolderBelowAge as a String — it must be an Int literal (21)
